content: {"assessment": "We have found a bimodal distribution in orbital eccentricity that persists even after controlling for detection method. However, the sample size for high-eccentricity planets is small (N=12) and I cannot determine whether this pattern reflects real dynamical processes or a measurement artifact.", "next_move": "INTERROGATE", "rationale": "The pattern is suggestive but ambiguous — domain expertise is needed to interpret whether tidal circularization timescales could explain the observed cutoff.", "status": "STUCK", "question_for_human": "I found a sharp cutoff in eccentricity at orbital periods below 5 days — all short-period planets have e < 0.05. Is this a known tidal circularization effect, or could it be a detection artifact from how RV orbits are fit?", "context": "After filtering to planets with measured eccentricities (N=72), I found that all 15 planets with P < 5 days have e < 0.05, while longer-period planets show a wide eccentricity spread. This could be tidal circularization (real physics) or a fitting prior in RV analysis (artifact)."}
